0.q: FvDi19-3 overexpression in Arabidopsis enhanced plant drought and salt tolerance by promoting stomatal closure, improving the plant's ability to scavenge reactive oxygen species.

1.q: FvDi19-3 overexpression in Arabidopsis enhanced plant drought and salt tolerance by promoting stomatal closure, improving the plant's ability to scavenge reactive oxygen species.

3.q: FvWRKY75 could promote the removal of ROS by improving the activities of antioxidant enzymes, thus reducing damage to plants caused by salt stress.

4.q: FvCRK5 transgenic plants had lower leaf yellowing rates than WT... leaves had higher chlorophyll content, accumulated lower ROS levels and MDA content, and had enhanced SOD and POD activities.

5.q: When receiving a salt stress signal, FvMYB114 can promote the expression of SOS1 and SOS3 through the SOS pathway.

8.q: Further experiments indicated that FvWRKY42 and FvMYB114 can activate the expression of FvDi19-3, and expression of these three genes is dependent on the ABA signaling pathway.

9.q: Further experiments indicated that FvWRKY42 and FvMYB114 can activate the expression of FvDi19-3, and expression of these three genes is dependent on the ABA signaling pathway.

10.q: FvWRKY75 can directly bind to the promoter of the FvCRK5 gene by recognizing the W-box element... the relative luciferase activity ... cotransformed with FvWRKY75 and pro-FvCRK5 was significantly stronger than that of the control.

11.q: FvWRKY75 can activate the promoter of FvCRK5 to influence its expression.
